pattern: A prefix that reads as a power of five in binary can only ever be followed by more such prefixes, so trying every split point and taking the best suffix result is a small enough search space to solve directly with backward DP.
hints: A chunk can only ever be valid if its first character is '1' -- any chunk starting with '0' is dead on arrival, so you can stop extending from that start position immediately.
For a fixed start position, growing the chunk one character at a time lets you maintain its numeric value incrementally (double and add the new bit) instead of re-parsing the substring each time.

Work backwards from the end of the string: the best count of pieces starting at position i is 1 plus the best count starting at the end of some valid chunk beginning at i, and the empty suffix costs zero pieces.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, single zero is impossible
shortest possible string, a single valid chunk
leading '1' then a lone '0' -- the '0' can never start a chunk
string starts with '0' so no split can ever begin
two copies of the binary form of five back to back

stress.description: random binary strings up to the maximum allowed length
stress.note: length is capped at 15 regardless of the requested size since that is the problem's own bound
